insights.insight: Question-format headlines ('Still deploying manually?') outperform statement headlines ('Deploy Apps Faster') by 34% CTR
Social creative fatigue onset at ~14 days for US developer segment. CTR declines 28% from peak after day 14. Bi-weekly refresh cadence recommended.
Headlines with 'no code' convert 41% better than 'AI-powered' for non-technical segment. They want reassurance they don't need to learn coding, not excitement about AI.
Brand search terms ('replit', 'replit agent') have 8.2x higher signup-to-paid rate vs generic terms ('ai code generator'). Non-brand traffic needs stronger post-click activation.
Activation emails with specific project templates ('Build a habit tracker', 'Create a landing page') convert 2.4x vs generic 'Explore Replit' CTAs.
Celebration emails ('Your app is live!') triggered after first deploy drive 31% more D7 retention than feature discovery emails sent on the same day.
Before/after transformation posts ('I described an app → here it is running') get 2.1x engagement vs feature announcement posts for non-technical audience.
Social proof numbers in ad description extensions ('Used by 85% of Fortune 500') lift conversion rate 18% across all segments.

Developer segment CPA on 'ai code editor' keywords spiked 45% in week of Mar 11 — likely Cursor increasing bids. Consider pivoting budget to 'build app' and 'deploy app' keywords where competition is lower.
India geo responds 40% better to 'free' in headline position 1 vs any other hook. Price sensitivity is the dominant purchase driver in this market.
In India student segment, 'learn to build' framing outperforms 'build without coding' by 67%. Students want skill acquisition, not just output.
Paid search creative fatigue is slower than social — ~21 days before CTR degradation. But degradation is sharper once it starts (35% drop in 5 days vs gradual social decline).
'Describe what you want. Watch it get built.' hook achieves 2.3x CTR vs feature-list hooks for non-technical segment. Emotional promise > rational feature list.
User-generated 'look what I built' content outperforms brand-produced content by 1.7x on engagement and 1.3x on conversion. Authenticity signal matters more than production quality.
Specific time savings ('Build & Deploy in Under 5 Min') beat vague speed claims ('Build Apps Faster') by 22% CTR. Specificity builds trust.
EU enterprise segment responds 2.8x better to 'SOC 2 compliant' in headline vs any other qualifier. Security is the top gating concern, not speed or AI capability.
India student traffic from social has high signup rate (4.2%) but very low first-app-created rate (0.8%). The funnel leaks at activation — creative attracts but product onboarding loses them.
Enterprise lifecycle emails with 'unblock your team' framing convert 2.1x vs 'AI-powered development' framing. Outcome framing > technology framing for enterprise buyers.

LATAM payback period drifted from 72 to 91 days over 2 weeks — approaching 90-day target. Low CPA but conversion-to-paid rate declining. May need to tighten audience targeting or adjust lifecycle messaging for LATAM.
App Store screenshots showing the design canvas (Agent 4 infinite canvas) drive 19% higher install rate vs screenshots showing code output. Visual builders want to see the visual tool.

India CPA spiked from $7.20 to $11.40 (+58%) over 7 days. Likely cause: competitor entering India paid search (possibly Bolt or Lovable). Recommend shifting 30% of India search budget to social where CPA remains stable at $5.80.
Adding 'Parallel AI Agents' to description copy lifted CTR 11% for developer segment. Developers respond to capability specifics, not general 'AI-powered' language.

meta_patterns.pattern: Geo-differentiated messaging is 1.8x more effective than global messaging on average
meta_patterns.implication: Always generate geo-specific variants, never one-size-fits-all

meta_patterns.pattern: Outcome framing outperforms technology framing across all segments except developers
meta_patterns.implication: Default to outcome framing; only use tech framing for developer segment

meta_patterns.pattern: Specificity beats generality in every A/B test where it was isolated
meta_patterns.implication: Never use vague claims. Always include specific numbers, times, or names.

meta_patterns.pattern: Non-technical segment is the highest-CTR, highest-growth audience across all channels
meta_patterns.implication: Disproportionate creative investment in non-technical messaging will yield best marginal returns
